reference: Hi, I met with an accident six years ago when I was seventeen, and it caused a deformity in my right leg. I was kept on traction for a few days and then plastered. I was told that the bending, which is around eight degrees, would fade away eventually, but it is still the same. I have no physical trouble with it, but it causes me mental disturbance when people look at me when I walk. Can it be corrected now?
text: Hi. I met with an accident 6 years ago when I was 17, and it caused a deformity in my right leg. I was kept on traction for a few days and then flattered. I was told that the bending, which is around 8 degrees, would fade away eventually, but it is still the same. I have no physical trouble with it, but it causes me mental disturbance when people look at me when I walk. Can it be corrected now?
